NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David. How long, O LORD? Will You forget me forever? How long will You hide Your face from me?
NKJV.2: How long shall I take counsel in my soul, Having sorrow in my heart daily? How long will my enemy be exalted over me?
NKJV.3: Consider and hear me, O LORD my God; Enlighten my eyes, Lest I sleep the sleep of death;
NKJV.4: Lest my enemy say, “I have prevailed against him”; Lest those who trouble me rejoice when I am moved.
NKJV.5: But I have trusted in Your mercy; My heart shall rejoice in Your salvation.
NKJV.6: I will sing to the LORD, Because He has dealt bountifully with me.

KJV.1: To the chief Musician<S>5329</S>, A Psalm<S>4210</S> of David<S>1732</S>. How long wilt thou forget<S>7911</S> me, O LORD<S>3068</S>? for ever<S>5331</S>? how long wilt thou hide<S>5641</S> thy face<S>6440</S> from me? <sup>chief: or, overseer</sup>
KJV.2: How long shall I take<S>7896</S> counsel<S>6098</S> in my soul<S>5315</S>, having sorrow<S>3015</S> in my heart<S>3824</S> daily<S>3119</S>? how long shall mine enemy<S>341</S> be exalted<S>7311</S> over me?
KJV.3: Consider<S>5027</S> and hear<S>6030</S> me, O LORD<S>3068</S> my God<S>430</S>: lighten<S>215</S> mine eyes<S>5869</S>, lest I sleep<S>3462</S> the sleep of death<S>4194</S>;
KJV.4: Lest mine enemy<S>341</S> say<S>559</S>, I have prevailed<S>3201</S> against him; and those that trouble<S>6862</S> me rejoice<S>1523</S> when I am moved<S>4131</S>.
KJV.5: But I have trusted<S>982</S> in thy mercy<S>2617</S>; my heart<S>3820</S> shall rejoice<S>1523</S> in thy salvation<S>3444</S>.
KJV.6: I will sing<S>7891</S> unto the LORD<S>3068</S>, because he hath dealt bountifully<S>1580</S> with me.

ESV.1: How long, O LORD? Will you forget me forever? How long will you hide your face from me?
ESV.2: How long must I take counsel in my soul and have sorrow in my heart all the day? How long shall my enemy be exalted over me?
ESV.3: Consider and answer me, O LORD my God; light up my eyes, lest I sleep the sleep of death,
ESV.4: lest my enemy say, “I have prevailed over him,” lest my foes rejoice because I am shaken.
ESV.5: But I have trusted in your steadfast love; my heart shall rejoice in your salvation.
ESV.6: I will sing to the LORD, because he has dealt bountifully with me.

NASB.1: For the choir director. A Psalm of David. How long, O LORD? Will You forget me forever? How long will You hide Your face from me?
NASB.2: How long shall I take counsel in my soul, [Having] sorrow in my heart all the day? How long will my enemy be exalted over me?
NASB.3: Consider [and] answer me, O LORD my God; Enlighten my eyes, or I will sleep the [sleep of] death,
NASB.4: And my enemy will say, 'I have overcome him,' [And] my adversaries will rejoice when I am shaken.
NASB.5: But I have trusted in Your lovingkindness; My heart shall rejoice in Your salvation.
NASB.6: I will sing to the LORD, Because He has dealt bountifully with me.

NLT.1:
O LORD, how long will you forget me? Forever?
How long will you look the other way?
NLT.2:
How long must I struggle with anguish in my soul,
with sorrow in my heart every day?
How long will my enemy have the upper hand?
NLT.3:
Turn and answer me, O LORD my God!
Restore the sparkle to my eyes, or I will die.
NLT.4:
Don’t let my enemies gloat, saying, “We have defeated him!”
Don’t let them rejoice at my downfall.
NLT.5:
But I trust in your unfailing love.
I will rejoice because you have rescued me.
NLT.6:
I will sing to the LORD
because he is good to me.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. A Psalm by David. How long, Yahweh? Will you forget me forever? How long will you hide your face from me?
WEB.2: How long shall I take counsel in my soul, having sorrow in my heart every day? How long shall my enemy triumph over me?
WEB.3: Behold, and answer me, Yahweh, my God. Give light to my eyes, lest I sleep in death;
WEB.4: Lest my enemy say, “I have prevailed against him”; Lest my adversaries rejoice when I fall.
WEB.5: But I trust in your loving kindness. My heart rejoices in your salvation.
WEB.6: I will sing to Yahweh, because he has been good to me.

CSB17.1:
For the choir director. A psalm of David. [1] How long, LORD? Will you forget me forever?
How long will you hide your face from me? ⓕ
CSB17.2:
How long will I store up anxious concerns [1] within me,
agony in my mind every day?
How long will my enemy dominate me? ⓖ
CSB17.3:
Consider me and answer, LORD my God.
Restore brightness to my eyes; ⓗ
otherwise, I will sleep in death.
CSB17.4:
My enemy will say, “I have triumphed over him,”
and my foes will rejoice because I am shaken. ⓘ
CSB17.5:
But I have trusted in your faithful love; ⓙ
my heart will rejoice in your deliverance. ⓚ
CSB17.6:
I will sing to the LORD
because he has treated me generously. ⓛ

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David. How long, O LORD? Will You forget me forever?
How long will You hide Your face from me?
AMP.2:
How long must I take counsel in my soul,
Having sorrow in my heart day after day?
How long will my enemy exalt himself and triumph over me?
AMP.3:
Consider and answer me, O LORD my God;
Give light (life) to my eyes, or I will sleep the sleep of death,
AMP.4:
And my enemy will say, "I have overcome him,"
And my adversaries will rejoice when I am shaken.
AMP.5:
But I have trusted and relied on and been confident in Your lovingkindness and faithfulness;
My heart shall rejoice and delight in Your salvation.
AMP.6:
I will sing to the LORD,
Because He has dealt bountifully with me.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
Long enough, God—
you've ignored me long enough.
I've looked at the back of your head
long enough.
MSG.2:
Long enough
I've carried this ton of trouble,
lived with a stomach full of pain.
Long enough my arrogant enemies
have looked down their noses at me.
MSG.3:
Take a good look at me, God, my God;
I want to look life in the eye,
MSG.4:
So no enemy can get the best of me
or laugh when I fall on my face.
MSG.5:
I've thrown myself headlong into your arms—
I'm celebrating your rescue.
MSG.6:
I'm singing at the top of my lungs,
I'm so full of answered prayers.

NIV.1:
Psalm 13
For the director of music. A psalm of David.
How long, O Lord? Will you forget me forever?
How long will you hide your face from me?
NIV.2:
How long must I wrestle with my thoughts
and every day have sorrow in my heart?
How long will my enemy triumph over me?
NIV.3:
Look on me and answer, O Lord my God.
Give light to my eyes, or I will sleep in death;
NIV.4:
my enemy will say, “I have overcome him,”
and my foes will rejoice when I fall.
NIV.5:
But I trust in your unfailing love;
my heart rejoices in your salvation.
NIV.6:
I will sing to the Lord,
for he has been good to me.

NRSVCE.1:
How long, O LORD? Will you forget me forever?
How long will you hide your face from me?
NRSVCE.2:
How long must I bear pain in my soul,
and have sorrow in my heart all day long?
How long shall my enemy be exalted over me?
NRSVCE.3:
Consider and answer me, O LORD my God!
Give light to my eyes, or I will sleep the sleep of death,
NRSVCE.4:
and my enemy will say, “I have prevailed”;
my foes will rejoice because I am shaken.
NRSVCE.5:
But I trusted in your steadfast love;
my heart shall rejoice in your salvation.
NRSVCE.6:
I will sing to the LORD,
because he has dealt bountifully with me.

NET.1: How long, LORD, will you continue to ignore me? How long will you pay no attention to me?
NET.2: How long must I worry, and suffer in broad daylight? How long will my enemy gloat over me?
NET.3: Look at me! Answer me, O LORD my God! Revive me, or else I will die!
NET.4: Then my enemy will say, “I have defeated him!” Then my foes will rejoice because I am upended.
NET.5: But I trust in your faithfulness. May I rejoice because of your deliverance!
NET.6: I will sing praises to the LORD when he vindicates me.
